strategies.summary: There is a tension between the strong feature redundancy observed and the presence of features with strong class-conditioned representational differences, indicating a complex relationship where some features may be redundant yet others capture critical class distinctions.
strategies.strategic_objective: Explore the coexistence of feature redundancy with strong class-conditioned representational differences to clarify their interplay and impact on model robustness.

strategies.summary: Features that are effectively constant coexist with related features showing high class-conditioned variance, raising questions about whether constants represent genuine signals, defaults, or measurement artifacts.
strategies.strategic_objective: Clarify the nature and role of effectively constant features coexisting with highly variable features to inform their treatment in modeling and interpretation.

strategies.summary: Packet-size and segment-size metrics along with destination port distributions show strong class-conditioned differences, suggesting these features may capture meaningful distinctions between benign and DDoS traffic classes.
strategies.strategic_objective: Investigate the stability and classification relevance of class-conditioned packet size and port distribution differences to understand their contribution to distinguishing traffic classes.
